title: Freshmen Orientation Day 2025
description: The Freshman Mentor Program (FMP) is a comprehensive advisory program that spans the length of freshman year and focuses on helping students create and maintain connections to campus, classmates and our community. The FMP upperclassman mentors are "trusted guides" who help students become familiar with Stevenson's physical layout, policies, programs, traditions and personnel, while simultaneously developing powerful relationships with each freshman. Each freshman will spend the day with their FMP mentors on Freshman Orientation Day, the first day of a year-long journey to help each freshman find success at Stevenson!For more information, visit the Incoming Freshman: Class of 2029 web page.

title: PTC Kickoff

description: Everyone is welcome-no experience required! Join PTC at our Kickoff Meeting to learn how you can get involved this season. Come see what we're all about and start your theatre journey!

This is a hands-on event where you can try drilling, sewing, programming lights and sound, and see what it takes to bring a show to life. All are welcome-no experience required!

title: Family Action Network presents: Dream School: Finding the College That's Right for You
description: Attending college has long been a rite of passage for millions of teens and a bedrock of the American dream. But that well-worn path has lately taken a wrong turn, denying admission even to super-achievers and putting intolerable stress on family finances. Now, in Dream School: Finding the College That's Right for You, Jeff Selingo (FAN '20, '21, '22) shifts the spotlight from how colleges pick students to how students can better pick colleges.With test-optional policies and grade inflation leveling the playing field for applicants, getting into prestigious schools has become a kind of lottery. Backed by unparalleled research-and an eye-opening survey of more than 3,000 parents-Dream School reveals what really matters in a college: strong job prospects after graduation, hands-on learning experiences, and a sense of belonging.LINKSEvent Page: https://www.familyactionnetwork.net/events/dream-s...Register via Zoom: http://www.bit.ly/Selingo25FANWebinar

title: Family Action Network presents: After the Spike: Population, Progress, and the Case for People
description: Perhaps you've noticed an uptick in media attention on low birth rates around the globe, and the myriad policies various countries have adopted to address declining population rates as an issue of societal stabilization -- living standards, caregiving, climate, material progress, gender equity. Geruso is a leading light in the field of economic demography, and the book takes a nuanced approach devoid of hysteria and hyperbole. A great topic that taps into core FAN buckets of "families and parenting," "work/life balance," "well-being," etc. Geruso's convo partner, Katy Milkman, is a named professor at Wharton, and co-founder and co-director, with Angela Duckworth (FAN '12, '16), of the Behavior Change for Good Initiative at Penn. She's a superstar, and she and Geruso will have a sparky, smart, thought-provoking evening.LINKSEvent Page: https://www.familyactionnetwork.net/events/after-t...Register via Zoom: https://us02web.zoom.us/webinar/register/411756571...

title: Family Action Network presents: Mastery: Why Deeper Learning is Essential in an Age of Distraction
description: Mastery learning is a hot topic, and this new book offers a survey of how it is being applied throughout the globe. FAN has hosted several speakers that look at the future of education, redesigning learning, alternatives to traditional classroom teaching. Wagner is widely respected, and although Christensen is new to us, his background is fascinating, and we're confident that this will be a horizon-opening event. We're pleased that Dr. Peter Tragos, New Trier HS D203's new superintendent, will be in conversation with the co-authors -- Peter mentioned that Tony's work is featured in his doctoral thesis. A great program for educators, school leaders, parents, and all those who appreciate looking into the future without fear.LINKSEvent Page: https://www.familyactionnetwork.net/events/mastery...Register via Zoom: https://us02web.zoom.us/webinar/register/591757288...

title: 2025 Fall Play: Macbeth
location: Stevenson High School | Black Box Theatre
description: Fair is foul, and foul is fair! If you were promised the world, is it your responsibility to claim it? For one infamous Scottish soldier, the prophecy that he will become king ignites a bloody path to the throne, at all costs. This Shakespearean classic from English class comes to life in a fast-moving 80-minute adaptation with sixteen actors playing 30+ roles, modern music, spooky sets, special effects, and costumes to die for. Join us in a dangerous world of war and witches to hear the beautiful, horrible, iconic story of MACBETH.🎟️ PURCHASE Tickets via GoFANAudience Advisory: The story of Macbeth includes dark themes, including war, murder, and witchcraft. This production is an immersive experience that includes audience interaction and theatrical elements such as fog, haze, flashing lights, and loud noises. Patrons who cannot comfortably stand for 15 minutes should speak with an usher about pre-seating.

title: CCC Presents: Financial Aid Night
location: Stevenson High School | Performing Arts Center & Zoom
description: The College Career Center will hold its annual Financial Aid Night at 7 p.m. on Monday, Oct. 6th, in the Little Theatre (next to the PAC) as well as live-streamed via Zoom (https://d125-org.zoom.us/j/98905126369). This event will be recorded, and the slides and recording will be available on the College Career Center website within a week of the event.Post Secondary Counselors Ms. Sara Hanson and Mr. Dan Miller will partner with Mr. Jerry Cebrzynski, Associate Vice President for Financial Aid at Lake Forest College, for this presentation, which is meant primarily for families who have current juniors or seniors.Details about applying for college financial assistance through the Free Application for Federal Student Aid (FAFSA) will be discussed, along with the types of financial aid available, and how eligibility for aid is determined. Families with seniors typically complete the 2026-27 FAFSA form in October.Junior and Senior families that would like to request a copy of the slide deck in another language are asked to e-mail: shs_CollegeCareer@d125.org, or contact the CCC at (847) 415-4517 by Monday, September 29th.

It is a great opportunity for families and students of all ages to learn about the options and services that are available after high school.Learn more at https://postsecondarychoices.org/

title: Family Action Network presents: Dealing with Feeling: Use Your Emotions to Create the Life You Want
description: In Dealing with Feeling: Use Your Emotions to Create the Life You Want, Marc Brackett, Ph.D. (FAN '19), offers an essential guide for regulating how we respond to our emotions to transform our lives.Success in virtually every aspect of life―career, friendship, love, and family―is determined mainly by one thing: how we deal with emotions. In your most challenging moments, how did you respond? Did you fly off the handle? Were you paralyzed by indecision? Did you engage in behaviors that undermined your best intentions? Or did you exhibit grace under pressure and flourish? How you responded likely shaped what happened next. But emotion regulation isn't a fixed trait, as many believe. It is a set of skills and strategies that must be learned, practiced, and refined over a lifetime.Brackett examines his own challenges dealing with emotions, explains the latest science of emotion regulation, and provides a set of practical, evidence-based strategies to help us use our feelings wisely to nurture healthy relationships, achieve ambitious goals, and experience greater well-being. At the heart of his message is acceptance: There are no bad emotions―only emotions we don't understand or know how to direct in positive, intentional ways.No registration required.LINKSEvent Page: https://www.familyactionnetwork.net/events/dealing...

title: SHS & Stand Strong Coalition present: How a Student's Community Fosters Wellness and Academic Success
description: This event is co-hosted by Stevenson High School and Stand Strong Coalition.Doug Bolton is a Licensed Clinical Psychologist and Director of School Consultation for Formative Psychological Services. Dr. Bolton provides consultation, supervision and professional development to parents, educators, and clinicians throughout the US to create communities that help foster student and staff resilience. His book, Untethered was released in March.Join us at prior to the event at 6 p.m. for a Resource Fair hosted by the SHS Peer Helpers.He is going to be discussing what he calls "Myths that Undermine Kids Development."Happiness comes not from money or material success, but from the quality of our relationships and sense of connection with others.Selective schools are not the ticket to success, true success is measured by how engaged, supported, and purposeful a student is wherever they learn.Achievement addiction occurs when self-worth depends solely on accomplishments, leading to stress and loss of balance.Children and adolescents do not develop at the same rate; each follows a unique path of cognitive, emotional, and social growth.

title: 2025 Fall Musical: Mary Poppins
location: Stevenson High School | Performing Arts Center
description: One of the most popular Disney movies of all time is capturing hearts in a whole new way: as a practically perfect musical! Based on the books by P.L. Travers and the classic Walt Disney film, Cameron Mackintosh and Disney's Mary Poppins delighted Broadway audiences for over 2,500 performances and received nominations for nine Olivier and seven Tony Awards, including Best Musical. The jack-of-all-trades, Bert, introduces us to England in 1910 and the troubled Banks family. Young Jane and Michael have sent many a nanny packing before Mary Poppins arrives on their doorstep. Using a combination of magic and common sense, she must teach the family members how to value each other again. Mary Poppins takes the children on many magical and memorable adventures, but Jane and Michael aren't the only ones upon whom she has a profound effect. title: Family Action Network presents: Parenting Anxiety: Breaking the Cycle of Worry and Raising Resilient Kids
description: Parenting is hard-especially for families navigating anxiety. Today's parents face an overwhelming volume of advice shaped by rigid cultural ideals of what "good" parenting looks like, particularly when it comes to managing children's fears and worries. How do you raise resilient children in an age of uncertainty? How do you parent effectively when you yourself are anxious? Much of the advice parents encounter, unfortunately, encourages responses that can increase the long-term risk of anxiety in children. In trying to protect kids from discomfort, well-intentioned parents may block experiences children need to build courage, confidence, and resilience.In her new book Parenting Anxiety: Breaking the Cycle of Worry and Raising Resilient Kids, Meredith Elkins, Ph.D., a clinical psychologist and faculty member at Harvard Medical School, brings clarity to this increasingly urgent conversation. Drawing on her clinical work with children and families-and her own experience as a parent-Dr. Elkins helps parents rethink so-called "negative" emotions such as fear, sadness, and guilt, and understand how modern parenting pressures contribute to anxiety in both children and adults. She encourages families to clarify their values and adopt a "love and limits" approach-balancing compassion with healthy boundaries while resisting the urge to shield children from every discomfort.LINKSEvent Page

title: 2026 Frosh/Soph Play: Too Much Light Makes the Baby Go Blind
description: The year's Freshmen/Sophomore play is a high-energy, audience-involved experience. The company has prepared 30 short plays that they hope to perform in under 60 minutes. The audience chooses the order of the play by calling out numbers as a timer counts down the minutes. Having opened in 1988, Too Much Light Makes the Baby Go Blind is the longest-running show in Chicago history. Our 30 plays are from a collection written and chosen by the creator, Greg Allen.The company chose the 30 plays in our production from a collection of 105 Neo-Futurist plays. Additionally, they participated in two days of workshops with a Neo-Futurist Ensemble Member to better understand and apply the Neo-Futurist tenets of presence and task.🎟️ PURCHASE Tickets via GoFAN: Feb. 5 | Feb. 6 | Feb. 7📖 DOWNLOAD: Event Program

title: Family Action Network: The Balancing Act: Creating Healthy Dependency and Connection Without Losing Yourself
description: Join us for this Family Action Network event, held via Zoom, on Tuesday, Feb. 26 at 7 p.m. Licensed therapist Nedra Glover Tawwab has built a remarkable platform exploring relationships and self-care-two New York Times bestsellers, 1.8 million Instagram followers, a weekly podcast, and a widely-read newsletter. Her newest book, The Balancing Act: Creating Healthy Dependency and Connection Without Losing Yourself, extends her groundbreaking work by offering a roadmap to relationships that honor both dependency and individuality.With signature clarity and honesty, Tawwab explores what it means to cultivate healthy dependency in contrast to the extremes of hyper-independence or codependency that many of us struggle with. The Balancing Act combines boundary-setting with building and maintaining relationships through genuine connection.Register for the event here: www.bit.ly/TawwabFANSponsor

title: CCC Presents: Career Ready, Trade & Military Expo
description: This event will highlight careers that have limited (if any) educational requirements after high school. While the Expo will be available for students of any grade level to visit, it will be especially well-suited for seniors seeking information from local businesses regarding full-time career opportunities, apprenticeships and advancement options. Most of the opportunities will require either on-the-job training, certificate programs (12-24 months) or associate degrees (no bachelor's degree required).Some sample careers that will be represented on this night include real estate agent, traditional trades (plumbing, HVAC, electrical, etc.), aviation, cosmetology, manufacturing, pharmacy technician, dental assistant, auto body repair, police, fire and EMT. There will also be representatives from the College of Lake County to discuss how their programming can help prepare students for some of these types of careers.You can check out out more information about our expo participants here. Feel free to contact the College Career Center with any questions.

title: 2026 Winter Play: Puffs, or Seven Increasingly Eventful Years at a Certain School of Magic and Magic
description: Get ready for a night of big laughs, surprising heart, and a whole lot of magic ✨Puffs is a fast-paced, hilarious, and surprisingly touching comedy that tells the story of the other students at a certain famous wizard school-the ones who weren't chosen as heroes, but showed up anyway. Full of clever humor, theatrical magic, and moments that genuinely hit home, this show celebrates friendship, perseverance, and finding your place, even when you feel overlooked.This production is a true ensemble event, with over 30 students onstage and another 30 students behind the scenes working together to create an epic theatrical experience. From inventive staging to quick-fire comedy, it's a celebration of teamwork, creativity, and student talent at every level.The show runs approximately 2 hours and 15 minutes and includes mature and atmospheric language. It is appropriate for most audiences and perfect for families, students, and anyone who loves comedy with heart.Whether you're a theatre fan, a fantasy lover, or just looking for a fun night out, Puffs promises laughs, surprises, and a whole lot of spirit. 🪄🎭PURCHASE TICKETS HERE.Puffs is not authorized, sanctioned, licensed or endorsed by J.K Rowling, Warner Bros. or any person or company associated with the Harry Potter books, films or play.Content Warning: Mature Language and atmospherics and haze will be used.

title: The AP and Advanced Art Show
description: The AP and Advanced Art Show exhibition will take place 7:30 a.m. - 3:30 p.m. on Tuesday April 14 and 7:30 am - 2:30 pm on Wednesday April 15 in Room 2104 and the Upper Forum. description: Every donation saves 3 lives! National Honor Society is hosting their spring blood drive on Friday, April 17, in the Wrestling Room. Sign up spots are available to donate blood from period 4 through 4:30 pm. Students are encouraged to sign up to donate during their lunch period or after school. They can also sign up during a class period but must also complete a field trip form and receive permission from their teacher to attend.Students must be 16 years of age to donateAll students participating must have a signed consent form.All students must complete a field trip form and receive permission from a teacher to attend. Students are to sign up to attend during their lunch/free period but need permission from the teacher after lunch/free period just in case the donation time runs over. (Example: I sign up to volunteer during my lunch 4th. I need a signature from my 5th hour teacher in case the donation runs into 5th.)

description: Wanna have a good laugh? Are you free at 7 p.m. on Friday April 17? title: NSC Art Festival
description: The North Suburban Conference Art Festival is an amazing, one-day event that celebrates the artistic achievements of high school students from across the region. Participating schools include Lake Zurich High School, Libertyville High School, Mundelein High School,Adlai E. Stevenson High School, Warren Township High School, Zion-Benton Township High School and Waukegan High School. Students selected for this prestigious festival are recognized for their outstanding dedication and talent in the visual arts. The festival provides these young artists an opportunity to showcase their work, compete, and connect with peers from other schools, making it a highlight of the year for the region's artistic community. This year's hosting school is Stevenson High School.

title: Family Engagement: How to Start: Discovering Your Life's Work
description: Work is how we spend much of our time. It's our engine of progress: how cancer therapies are invented, political campaigns won, thrilling art created and matched with an audience. Instead of letting cynicism take over, Kantor identifies two principles to help young people discover their life's work: craft and need. By pairing the two, they can navigate tough, sensitive choices: how to think about money. How much risk to take on. When to buck what others are saying.Powerful and provocative, How to Start is a statement of faith for young people as they make their way through uncertain times, offering wisdom, strategy, and a set of aspirations to launch their careers and last their whole lives.To register: www.bit.ly/KantorFANSponsor

title: Mélange and Repertory General Auditions
description: General auditions for Mélange and Repertory Dance Companies will be held May 13-14 from 3:45-6 p.m. in Room 2112. Be sure to sign up in advance. Come prepared by learning the audition phrase (released May 8) and creating a strong 45-60 second original solo in jazz or contemporary. Results will be posted the week of May 18th, and all selected dancers are expected to attend a mandatory meeting on May 19. Company placement will take place in August, with dancers cast into either Mélange or Repertory at that time.
